code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test02-AUG-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is brown, cloudy, with ammoniacal odor, and contains glucose, bilirubin, and ketones, but no nitrites, leukocytes, or hematuria. These findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and renal tubular dysfunction. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
